description: Basic date and time types. Supplies classes for manipulating dates and times in both simple and complex ways, with support for timezone-aware arithmetic.

classes.name: datetime
classes.description: A combination of a date and a time. Attributes: year, month, day, hour, minute, second, microsecond, tzinfo.

constructors.signature: datetime(year, month, day, hour=0, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0, tzinfo=None)

params.name: year
params.description: Year between MINYEAR and MAXYEAR inclusive

params.name: day
params.description: Day between 1 and the number of days in the given month/year

constructors.description: Create a new datetime instance with the given date and time attributes.

methods.name: now

params.name: tz
params.description: Timezone object. If None, returns local date and time.

returns.description: Current local datetime, or timezone-aware datetime if tz is provided

methods.description: Return the current local date and time. If optional argument tz is not None, the current date and time is converted to tz's time zone.

methods.name: utcnow

returns.description: Current UTC datetime (naive, without tzinfo)

methods.description: Return the current UTC date and time, with tzinfo None. This is like now() but returns the current UTC date and time as a naive datetime object.

methods.name: fromtimestamp

params.name: timestamp
params.description: POSIX timestamp (seconds since epoch, as returned by time.time())

params.name: tz
params.description: Timezone object. If None, the timestamp is converted to the platform's local date and time.

returns.description: Local datetime corresponding to the timestamp

methods.description: Return the local date and time corresponding to the POSIX timestamp.

methods.name: strptime

params.name: date_string
params.description: String representing a date and time

returns.description: datetime parsed from the string according to the format

methods.description: Return a datetime corresponding to date_string, parsed according to the format string. Raises ValueError if the string cannot be parsed.

methods.name: combine

returns.description: A new datetime with date components from the date argument and time components from the time argument

methods.description: Return a new datetime object whose date components are equal to the given date object's, and whose time components and tzinfo are equal to the given time object's.

methods.name: today

returns.description: Current local datetime with tzinfo None

methods.description: Return the current local datetime, with tzinfo None. Equivalent to datetime.fromtimestamp(time.time()).

methods.name: strftime

params.name: format
params.description: Format string using directives like %Y (4-digit year), %m (month), %d (day), %H (hour), %M (minute), %S (second)

returns.description: String representation of the datetime according to the format

methods.description: Return a string representing the date and time, controlled by an explicit format string.

methods.name: date

returns.description: date object with same year, month, and day

methods.description: Return a date object with the same year, month, and day.

methods.name: time

returns.description: time object with same hour, minute, second, microsecond, and tzinfo None

methods.description: Return a time object with the same hour, minute, second, and microsecond. tzinfo is None.

methods.name: replace
methods.signature: replace(year=None, month=None, day=None, hour=None, minute=None, second=None, microsecond=None, tzinfo=None)

returns.description: New datetime with the specified fields replaced

methods.description: Return a datetime with the same attributes, except for those attributes given new values by whichever keyword arguments are specified.

methods.name: isoformat

params.name: sep
params.description: Separator between date and time portions (default 'T')

returns.description: String in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS[.mmmmmm][+HH:MM]

methods.description: Return a string representing the date and time in ISO 8601 format.

methods.name: timetuple

returns.description: A time.struct_time such as returned by time.localtime()

methods.description: Return a time.struct_time such as returned by time.localtime(). dst flag is set to -1.

methods.name: weekday

returns.description: Day of the week as an integer (Monday is 0, Sunday is 6)

methods.description: Return the day of the week as an integer, where Monday is 0 and Sunday is 6.

methods.name: __add__

returns.description: New datetime offset by the timedelta

methods.description: Return a datetime that is timedelta duration after this datetime. Equivalent to datetime + timedelta.

methods.name: __sub__

params.name: other
params.description: A datetime (returns timedelta) or timedelta (returns datetime)

returns.description: timedelta if subtracting a datetime, datetime if subtracting a timedelta

methods.description: If other is a timedelta, returns a datetime that is timedelta duration before this datetime. If other is a datetime, returns the timedelta between the two.

fields.name: year
fields.description: Year between MINYEAR and MAXYEAR inclusive

fields.name: day
fields.description: Day between 1 and the number of days in the given month of the given year

fields.name: tzinfo
fields.description: The timezone information object or None

fields.name: resolution
fields.description: The smallest possible difference between non-equal datetime objects: timedelta(microseconds=1)

classes.name: date
classes.description: An idealized date, assuming the current Gregorian calendar always was, and always will be, in effect. Attributes: year, month, day.

params.name: year
params.description: Year between MINYEAR and MAXYEAR inclusive

params.name: day
params.description: Day between 1 and the number of days in the given month/year

constructors.description: Create a new date instance with the given year, month, and day.

methods.name: today

returns.description: Current local date

methods.description: Return the current local date.

methods.name: fromtimestamp

returns.description: Local date corresponding to the timestamp

methods.description: Return the local date corresponding to the POSIX timestamp.

methods.name: fromordinal

params.name: ordinal
params.description: Proleptic Gregorian ordinal, where January 1 of year 1 has ordinal 1

returns.description: Date corresponding to the proleptic Gregorian ordinal

methods.description: Return the date corresponding to the proleptic Gregorian ordinal.

methods.name: strftime

returns.description: String representation of the date according to the format

methods.description: Return a string representing the date, controlled by an explicit format string.

methods.name: isoformat

returns.description: String in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD

methods.description: Return a string representing the date in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD.

methods.name: timetuple

returns.description: A time.struct_time with time fields set to zero

methods.description: Return a time.struct_time such as returned by time.localtime(). Hours, minutes, and seconds are 0, and the DST flag is -1.

methods.name: weekday

returns.description: Day of the week (Monday is 0, Sunday is 6)

methods.description: Return the day of the week as an integer, where Monday is 0 and Sunday is 6.

methods.name: replace

returns.description: New date with the specified fields replaced

methods.description: Return a date with the same value, except for those parameters given new values by whichever keyword arguments are specified.

fields.name: year
fields.description: Year between MINYEAR and MAXYEAR inclusive

fields.name: day
fields.description: Day between 1 and the number of days in the given month/year

fields.name: resolution
fields.description: The smallest possible difference between non-equal date objects: timedelta(days=1)

classes.name: time
classes.description: An idealized time, independent of any particular day, assuming every day has exactly 24*60*60 seconds. Attributes: hour, minute, second, microsecond, tzinfo.

methods.name: strftime

returns.description: String representation of the time according to the format

methods.description: Return a string representing the time, controlled by an explicit format string.

methods.name: isoformat

returns.description: String in ISO 8601 format: HH:MM:SS[.mmmmmm][+HH:MM]

methods.description: Return a string representing the time in ISO 8601 format: HH:MM:SS.mmmmmm or, if microsecond is 0, HH:MM:SS.

methods.name: replace

returns.description: New time with the specified fields replaced

methods.description: Return a time with the same value, except for those attributes given new values by whichever keyword arguments are specified.

fields.name: tzinfo
fields.description: The timezone information object or None

fields.name: resolution
fields.description: The smallest possible difference between non-equal time objects: timedelta(microseconds=1)

classes.name: timedelta
classes.description: A duration expressing the difference between two dates, times, or datetimes. Supports addition, subtraction, multiplication, and comparison.

constructors.signature: timedelta(days=0, seconds=0, microseconds=0, milliseconds=0, minutes=0, hours=0, weeks=0)

params.name: milliseconds
params.description: Number of milliseconds (converted to microseconds internally)

params.name: minutes
params.description: Number of minutes (converted to seconds internally)

params.name: hours
params.description: Number of hours (converted to seconds internally)

params.name: weeks
params.description: Number of weeks (converted to days internally)

constructors.description: Create a timedelta object representing a duration. Only days, seconds, and microseconds are stored internally. All arguments are converted to those three units.

methods.name: total_seconds

returns.description: Total number of seconds contained in the duration

methods.description: Return the total number of seconds contained in the duration. Equivalent to (td.microseconds + (td.seconds + td.days * 24 * 3600) * 10**6) / 10**6.

fields.name: min
fields.description: The most negative timedelta object: timedelta(-999999999)

fields.name: max
fields.description: The most positive timedelta object: timedelta(days=999999999, hours=23, minutes=59, seconds=59, microseconds=999999)

fields.name: resolution
fields.description: The smallest possible difference between non-equal timedelta objects: timedelta(microseconds=1)

constants.name: MINYEAR
constants.description: The smallest year number allowed in a date or datetime object. MINYEAR is 1.

constants.name: MAXYEAR
constants.description: The largest year number allowed in a date or datetime object. MAXYEAR is 9999.
